description_long: This is a classic blue and light gray biplane with a streamlined fuselage and two sets of straight, parallel wings connected by struts and wires. The aircraft features a forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a clear canopy, and the registration 'OO-JFN' is prominently displayed on the fuselage and upper wing. The tail section consists of a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer, both painted in matching blue and gray. The landing gear is fixed and consists of two main wheels under the lower wing and a small tailwheel. The engine is mounted at the nose, with a visible propeller. The overall design is reminiscent of early to mid-20th-century civil or training aircraft.
description: A blue and gray biplane with a forward enclosed cockpit, fixed landing gear, and a nose-mounted propeller engine.
description_view_0: Side view showing the blue fuselage, registration 'OO-JFN', and the two stacked wings with visible struts and wires.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the twin wings, nose-mounted propeller, and the fixed main landing gear beneath the lower wing.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same features as view 0, with the registration and tail details visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the tail assembly, single vertical stabilizer, and the alignment of the wings and landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings connected by struts and wires; no winglets or noticeable dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a single tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a clear canopy, likely single or tandem seat.
